data.title: J.P. Morgan reiterates Overweight rating on HBAN, downgrades M&T Bank
data.description:
J.P. Morgan reiterated its Overweight rating on Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN), while downgraded M&T Bank (NYSE:MTB) to Neutral.

For HBAN, the analysts see an upside to 2025 EPS driven by better than expected net interest income (NII) trends.

"There has been some concern about HBAN’s perceived asset sensitivity, and hence concern about weaker NII trends with Federal Reserve's rate cuts," said J.P. Morgan, and added it views these concerns as overdone given its deposit mix and strategy.

The analysts also see the headwind around receive fixed swaps becoming active in 2025 as manageable.

"We forecast upside to 2025 NII despite a more conservative view on loan growth versus the Street...longer term, HBAN should also hold up better if the economy slows given its more conservative credit risk profile," J.P. Morgan said in a note.

J.P. Morgan also raised the target price to $20 from $18.5.

Shares in the company were up 0.8% in premarket trade.

SA analysts rate the company at Buy, while SA quant rates it at Hold.

"We view MTB’s earnings outlook as solid, with large benefit in 2025 from roll off of receive fixed swaps driving sizable uplift to net interest income (NII)," the analysts said.

However, J.P. Morgan expects its office CRE exposure and relatively lower loan loss reserves to remain a headwind.

The analysts said it is moving to the sidelines on MTB as they do not see a catalyst for continued outperformance.

"It is in a strong position to ramp up buybacks in 2025, and should benefit in future stress tests from reduction of CRE exposure," the analysts said.

J.P. Morgan expects office CRE charge-offs to take time to resolve and are more constructive than the Street on the outlook for provisions and net charge-offs.

Shares in the company were down 0.6% in premarket trade.

SA quant rates the company at Strong Buy, while SA analysts rate it at Hold.

data.title: Pure Storage soars after 'robust' Q3, hyperscaler win keep analysts bullish
data.description:
Pure Storage (NYSE:PSTG) stock soared about 24% premarket on Wednesday after fiscal third-quarter results and guidance beat estimates and analysts maintained their bullish views on the company.

Piper Sandler upgraded Pure Storage's rating to Overweight from Neutral and raised the price target on the stock to $76 from $56.

Analysts led by James Fish said that Pure Storage's fiscal third quarter results were better than expected given traditional-sales, but lead metrics struggled.

However, they upgraded the stock, noting that a significant design win with a top-four cloud hyperscaler removes risk and creates a catalyst for upside ahead.

The analysts said that, the opportunity with this hyperscaler alone represents a more than $500M opportunity in 2026, more hyperscalers could be coming, and the narrative shifts to Cloud and AI over STaaS (Storage as a Service). Without this win and given lead-metrics' miss, the stock would have likely been down, the analysts added.

Other reasons for the upgrade, according to the analysts, are — Pure Storage has differentiation, proven by the large software-win; Flash is gaining share, including with cloud-players now around AI use-cases; and the STaaS transition has paused, effectively removing a revenue headwind.

In addition, the analysts see additional estimate upside and room for multiple expansion given the narrative shift.

Wells Fargo Securities kept its Overweight rating on the shares and raised the price target to $80 from $75.

Analysts Aaron Rakers and his team said that Pure Storage's shares are trading significantly higher following the announcement that the company has secured a design win at a top-4 hyperscaler - reinforcing their belief in the company's software-driven architectural differentiation as a key differentiator to such a design win. The company also noted discussions with several other hyperscalers.

The analysts said that Pure Storage expects minimal revenue contributions in 2025; materializing licensing-only (no hardware) revenue into 2026 with double-digits Exabytes ship tied to next-gen data center design/deploy.

J.P. Morgan maintained its Overweight rating and increased the price target on the shares to $75 from $60.

Analysts led by Pinjalim Bora said Pure Storage delivered a robust quarter with fiscal third results landing above expectations. The main highlight of Pure Storage's call was that the company signed a design win with a top four hyperscaler, which means that the hyperscaler will use Pure Storage's technology for all online storage needs, and that all future data centers for this hyperscaler are now approved to use Pure's technology as the standard for data storage, underscoring the company's strong differentiation in the market.

Pure expects early field trial buildouts to start next year, with large full production deployments, on the order of double-digit Exabytes, expected in 2026 (the company's fiscal 2027), with revenue and operating margin expansion to follow at that time, according to the analysts.

Bora and his team think that the stock is likely rising as investors look to digest the implications of the hyperscaler deal, although it will likely take a few quarters to understand the exact scope and size of the deal. Nevertheless, it lays the foundation for what the analysts think could be a very material growth lever for Pure Storage for years to come.

In addition, the analysts said that the deal further validates their medium term thesis that Pure Storage continues to distinguish itself from peers from a product innovation standpoint, especially as it embarks on its goal to expand the capacity of its DirectFlash, or DFM, modules over the next few years.

Related stocks: NetApp (NTAP) +1%, Nutanix (NTNX) +1% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Aramco, SLB and Linde partner to build huge Saudi carbon capture hub
data.description:
Saudi Aramco (ARMCO), oilfield services company SLB (NYSE:SLB) and industrial gases company Linde (LIN) said Wednesday they signed an agreement to build a carbon capture and storage project at its main refining and chemicals hub in Jubail, Saudi Arabia.

The project, which is expected to become one of the world's largest CCS hubs, will be 60% owned by Aramco (ARMCO), while SLB (SLB) and Linde (LIN) will hold 20% each, the companies said; financial terms were not disclosed.

The project's Phase 1 is expected to be complete by the end of 2027, capturing and storing up to 9M metric tons/year of carbon dioxide.

The Jubail CCS project is considered a key to Aramco's (ARMCO) plans to use a large amount of the natural gas from its giant Jafurah development to produce blue hydrogen and ammonia.

The fuels, which can be exported and used for heat or power, depend on converting natural gas to hydrogen and sequestering the carbon emissions to meet environmental standards.

data.title: Stock index futures rise after strong earnings reports, attention now on Powell's speech
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Wednesday, after strong earnings reports from Salesforce (CRM) and Marvell (MRVL) cheered investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.7% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.27%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.21%.

The November U.S. private nonfarm payrolls' rise fell short of consensus.

Wall Street's major equity averages finished mixed Tuesday, with tech stocks leading two key indexes to fresh record highs, while traders bumped up odds of a December rate cut following a stronger-than-expected data reading from the labor market.

"There was a clear intra-day selloff for U.S. Treasuries, after the latest JOLTS report showed a tighter labor market than expected," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Markets were fairly steady overall, with investors looking forward to the US jobs report on Friday as the next catalyst, Reid added.

Marvell (MRVL) rose 12.9% in premarket trade after the Mark Murphy-led semiconductor reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, due in part to artificial intelligence.

Salesforce (CRM) shares climbed higher in premarket trade, even after the firm delivered somewhat mixed results and guidance.

Investors will turn their attention to the Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell's speech later in the day.

Traders were also awaiting some macroeconomic data, including the November S&P global composite PMI, which is forecasted to come in at 55.3.

The October factory orders and the November ISM service index will land during market hours. The former is seen to rise 0.3% M/M, while the latter is anticipated to come in at 55.5.

data.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific completes $1B share repurchase
data.description:
• Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE:TMO) has completed the repurchase of $1B (1.9M shares) of its common stock.
  • As of Dec. 3, 2024, $3B remained available under the company’s share repurchase authorization.

data.title: Trisura Group announces Normal Course Issuer Bid
data.description:
• Trisura Group (TSX:TSU:CA) has filed its intention to make a Normal Course Issuer Bid to be transacted through the facilities of the TSX and/or alternative trading systems.
  • The notice provides that Trisura Group may, during the 12-month period commencing Dec. 6, 2024, and ending Dec. 5, 2025, purchase up to 1.43M common shares in total, representing ~3% of Trisura Group’s issued and outstanding common shares.
  • As at Nov. 22, 2024, Trisura Group had 47.8M common shares issued and outstanding.
  • Under the NCIB, Trisura Group may purchase up to 17,689 common shares on the TSX during any trading day, which represents 25% of the average daily trading volume of 70,758 common shares on the TSX for the six months ended Nov. 30, 2024.

data.title: ReTo stock climbs on production line contract with Middle Eastern client
data.description:
• ReTo Eco-Solutions (NASDAQ:RETO) Wednesday announced that its subsidiary, Beijing REIT Equipment Technology Co., entered into a sales contract of approximately $2 million with a client in the United Arab Emirates for the sale of RT18A, a fully automatic concrete block machine.
  • The RT18A, a newly developed large-scale, fully automatic concrete block production line manufactured by REIT Equipment, offers reliable and stable operation, allowing for production of a wide range of products.
  • RETO +4.38% premarket to $0.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cross Country Healthcare stock soars on acquisition by Aya Healthcare for $18.61/share
data.description:
• Aya Healthcare and Cross Country Healthcare (NASDAQ:CCRN) has entered into a definitive agreement whereby Aya will acquire Cross Country for $18.61/share in cash in a transaction valued at ~$615M.
  • The all-cash transaction represents a premium of 67% to Cross Country’s closing price on Dec. 3, 2024, and a premium of 68% to the volume-weighted average trading price for the 30-day trading period ended Dec. 3, 2024.
  • Aya and Cross Country offer complementary, tech-enabled workforce solutions across the continuum of care.
  • The proposed combination will diversify Aya’s coverage to include Cross Country’s clinical services in non-clinical settings, including schools and homes, in addition to travel nursing and allied health, per diem, permanent staff hiring, interim leadership, locum tenens and non-clinical professionals in all 50 states.
  • "By combining our strengths, resources and unwavering commitment to delivering best-in-class talent solutions, we are uniquely positioned to offer enhanced value to our healthcare systems, schools, clinicians and non-clinical professionals." said Alan Braynin, president and CEO of Aya.
  • Following completion of the transaction, and to ensure a seamless transition, Mr. Martins will continue to serve as President and CEO of Cross Country, driving the next phase of growth and innovation for Cross Country as part of Aya.
  • CCRN +61.56% premarket to $18.03.
